aliases: why is this topic route suppressed for some prompts
thumbs up down on a routed topic banner
blurb: Query-local signed exemplars that suppress or protect a routed topic banner for prompts like the ones it failed/passed on.
intent: Explain regin's signed query-exemplar mechanism as it operates over authoritative-topic routing — the contextual, query-local complement to the global, slow `importance` decay. An exemplar is the embedding of a prompt a topic banner was injected on, tagged `polarity` +1 (protect) or -1 (suppress) and `source` `auto`/`manual`; `topic_exemplars` keeps them per (topic, polarity), each polarity capped. At route time `store.topic_route_suppressed` withholds a banner query-locally when the prompt's cosine to the topic's negatives clears `topic_negative_suppress_sim` unless a positive protects it or a human `allowed` pin overrides. Covers the auto write path (`apply_topic_relevance`/`_record_topic_exemplars` → `add_topic_negatives`/`add_topic_positives`), the manual curate path (`/api/memory/exemplars`, `ExemplarCaseList.vue`, `regin memory exemplar-*`), the route-signal probe (`topic_query_signals`), and the inspect/per-row revert surfaces. The route-decision half is `memory-topic-route-feedback`; the design note is `docs/agent-memory-exemplars.md`.
label: Signed query exemplars — topic-route suppression/protection (auto & hand-curated)
